amber.description: Catalysts, regulatory proteins, molecular machines, receptors

black.description: Terminal products, cellular outcomes, equilibrium states, system endpoints

darkSkyBlue.description: Biochemical reactions, transport, modifications, transcription, translation

green.description: External signals, nutrient availability, environmental conditions, initial states

lightCyan.description: Metabolites, molecular complexes, cellular states, compounds

purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

yellow.description: Decision points with alternative pathways (yes/no branching)

description: Nucleotide excision repair (NER) is the primary pathway for removing bulky DNA lesions such as UV-induced pyrimidine dimers, chemical adducts, and other helix-distorting damage. In yeast, NER operates through two sub-pathways: global genome NER (GG-NER) for genome-wide surveillance and transcription-coupled NER (TC-NER) for rapid repair of transcribed genes. The pathway involves damage recognition, DNA unwinding, dual incision to excise the damaged strand, gap filling by DNA polymerase, and ligation. This process demonstrates sophisticated damage sensing, pathway choice logic, and coordination with transcription and cell cycle checkpoints.

in Bubble?} V -->|Yes| W[RPA Coating ssDNA] W --> X[Rad1-Rad10 Recruitment] X --> Y[Rad2 XPG Recruitment] Y --> ANDGATE1{Both Nucleases
Positioned?} ANDGATE1 -->|Yes| Z[Dual Incision] %% 5' and 3' Incisions Z --> AA[Rad1-Rad10 5' Incision] Z --> AB[Rad2 3' Incision] AA --> AC[Damaged Oligonucleotide Release] AB --> AC AC --> AD[24-32 nt Gap in DNA] %% Gap Filling AD --> AE{Repair Synthesis
Factors Ready?} AE --> ANDGATE2{Gap Present AND
Pol δ/ε Available?} ANDGATE2 -->|Yes| AF[PCNA Loading] AF --> AG[DNA Polymerase δ or ε] AG --> AH[Gap Filling Synthesis] AH --> AI[New DNA Strand] %% Ligation AI --> AJ{Nick Sealed?} AJ --> ANDGATE3{Synthesis Complete AND
Ligase Available?} ANDGATE3 -->|Yes| AK[DNA Ligase I] AK --> AL[Nick Sealing] AL --> AM[Restored DNA Duplex] %% Outcomes AM --> AN[Repaired DNA] AN --> AO{In Transcribed
Gene?} AO -->|Yes| AP[Transcription Resumption] AO -->|No| AQ[Genome Integrity Restored] AP --> AR[mRNA Synthesis Continues] AQ --> AS[Cell Cycle Progression] %% Failed Repair V -->|No| AT[Repair name: Nucleotide Excision Repair (NER) Pathway
scientificAccuracy: This flowchart is based on extensive genetic and biochemical studies of yeast NER, including the identification of RAD genes, structural analysis of repair complexes, and mechanistic studies of damage recognition and excision. The pathway represents the current understanding of how Rad4-Rad23 (XPC-RAD23B in humans) recognizes damage in GG-NER, how Rad26 (CSB) couples repair to transcription in TC-NER, and how the Rad1-Rad10 and Rad2 nucleases perform dual incision. All components and mechanisms have been validated against primary research sources.

sources.title: Nucleotide Excision Repair in Eukaryotes
sources.authors: Marteijn JA, Marteijn J, Vermeulen W, Scharer OD.

sources.title: The Yeast Rad25/Ssl2 Helicase Is a Component of the Transcription Factor TFIIH and Is Required for Nucleotide Excision Repair
sources.authors: Guzder SN, Habraken Y, Sung P, Prakash L, Prakash S.
